description: Figure 1 presents the hydrogen thermal desorption analysis (TDA) curve for a hydrogen-charged austenitic Fe30Mn6.5Al0.3C low-density steel specimen. The graph plots hydrogen desorption rate (mass ppm/min) against temperature (K), revealing a single distinct desorption peak centered at approximately 700 K with a maximum desorption rate of about 0.58 mass ppm/min. The curve was obtained via gas chromatography TDA using a thermal conductivity detector at a heating rate of 100 K/h. The sample was charged with hydrogen at 100 MPa and 543 K for 200 hours, resulting in a total measured hydrogen concentration of 101 mass ppm, while the uncharged reference contains less than 5 mass ppm. The single peak profile indicates that hydrogen exists primarily in solid solution (lattice diffusion) without significant trapping at microstructural defects such as grain boundaries or precipitates. This confirms the uniform distribution of hydrogen throughout the specimen prior to tensile testing, establishing the baseline hydrogen content for investigating hydrogen-enhanced deformation mechanisms and hydrogen embrittlement resistance in this steel.
